articles.title: One week after Israel's deadly strikes in Beirut, a family searches for their daughter
articles.summary: A week after Israel struck more than 100 times in 10 minutes across Lebanon, killing over 350 people, we spend time with a family still searching for their daughter in the rubble in central Beirut.

articles.title: Labor Department Investigates Texts Sent Among Staff, Secretary and Her Family
articles.summary: Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer asked staff members to bring wine to her hotel room, and to keep in touch with her husband and father.

articles.title: Middle East conflict causes a fluoride shortage for US drinking water
articles.summary: Some U.S. water systems are cutting back on fluoride because of a key chemical is in short supply. Israel is one of its main producers.

articles.title: Stocks Hit Record High as Wall St. Looks Beyond War
articles.summary: Investors appear to be treating an end to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran as a foregone conclusion, as the S&P 500 closes above 7,000.

articles.title: Popes have spoken out on politics before. But with Trump and Pope Leo it's different
articles.summary: President Trump's attacks on Pope Leo are unprecedented, religious experts told NPR. Here's how the situation differs from other popes' political critiques.

articles.title: Trump’s Quest for More Sway Over Fed Faces Fresh Hurdles
articles.summary: Jerome H. Powell’s term as chair of the Federal Reserve ends on May 15, but it appears increasingly likely that he will stay on in some capacity at the central bank after that.

articles.title: Debt Alarms Ring as Countries Rack Up More Emergency Spending
articles.summary: Amid signs of a prolonged period of high energy costs, policymakers are urging restraint as governments open up the public till to protect households and businesses.

articles.title: Ad Companies Settle With F.T.C. Over Claims of Harm to Conservative Sites
articles.summary: WPP, Dentsu and Publicis settled claims they colluded on policies to combat misinformation, denying ad revenue to publishers on the right.

articles.title: Amid Iran War, Companies Look to Extend a Record Profit Run by Raising Prices
articles.summary: Higher inflation is leading companies to raise prices without sacrificing margins.

articles.title: Visionary NPR leader Kevin Klose, who led network to new heights, has died
articles.summary: Klose led NPR for a decade starting in 1998, a period of incredible growth for the public media network.

articles.title: Once again, Trump threatens to fire Fed Chair Jerome Powell
articles.summary: President Trump once again threatened to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and doubled down on a discredited probe of the central bank.

articles.title: Trump’s Go-To Justification for Contentious Decisions: National Security
articles.summary: The administration has invoked national security in a variety of matters, including the White House ballroom and offshore wind farms, drawing rebukes from some judges.

articles.title: Trump Breaks With Meloni, Italy’s Leader, Amid Dispute Over Pope and Iran
articles.summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of President Trump’s closest European allies. Their friendship now appears in danger.

articles.title: Greetings from Nairobi, where taking a matatu is no ordinary bus ride
articles.summary: The moment you board, the music grabs you. These privately owned, brightly painted minibuses are moving canvases, mobile sound systems — rolling declarations of what young Nairobi finds cool.

articles.title: Trump administration moves to erase Jan. 6 riot convictions for seditious conspiracy
articles.summary: The Trump administration is moving to vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions of extremists involved in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack who earlier received commutations instead of full pardons.

articles.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
articles.summary: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

articles.title: Trump says Iran talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
articles.summary: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

articles.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
articles.summary: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

articles.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.

articles.title: 'Humiliated, broken, powerless': Sudan enters fourth year of war
articles.summary: While parts of Sudan's capital show fragile signs of life, across the country the conflict between the army and a rival paramilitary continues to drive mass displacement, hunger and allegations of atr

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
